content.assessment: This proposal's focus on choice, zoning, and maintainer-exemption levers is strongly consistent with the Finnish post-1990s lesson: comprehensive structural status (no formal early tracking) is not sufficient to prevent segregation if choice, commuting, and geographic sorting mechanisms remain unaddressed. The Swedish comparison further reinforces that institutional guardrails on choice matter independently of tracking age. However, since Hungary's proposal leaves the track age at 10 (the OECD's most extreme early-selection contexts), the Finnish lens would flag that this reform addresses a necessary but, by the lens's own logic, likely insufficient condition on its own.
opportunities: Combining this proposal with delayed selection (as the pilot proposal already suggests) would align more closely with both the Finnish success conditions and the guardrail lessons from Finland's and Sweden's post-1990s deregulation experiences
strengths: Directly targets the choice/commuting/zoning mechanisms that Finland's post-1990s experience identifies as sufficient to reintroduce segregation even in a formally comprehensive system, showing this lever matters independently of tracking age
Sweden comparison suggests institutional guardrails (akin to nearby-school enrollment defaults, funding equalization) can dampen, even if not eliminate, choice-driven segregation
threats: Given that Finland's own guardrails only dampened, not eliminated, choice-driven segregation, and Sweden's weaker guardrails produced steeper segregation increases, zoning/choice reform alone in Hungary carries a comparable risk of being an insufficient standalone intervention, especially given centralization constraints limiting local zoning adaptation
weaknesses: Leaves the age-10 tracking mechanism untouched, which the broader comprehensive-schooling literature associates with higher inequality of opportunity independent of choice effects, so this reform addresses only part of the segregation-generating architecture identified by the lens
